methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Galadyne is a pre-seed stage company in Defense & Security founded by Chandler Luzsicza, having raised $4.8M to date. The Defense & Security sector comprises 10 comparable firms: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems.

Only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $4.8M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for pre-seed to next-stage advancement, yielding $12M. Peer-Median triangulation could not be applied—while 19 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier comparables; a broad-sector median would lack sufficient precision for pre-seed staging.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $10M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. This range reflects stage-appropriate progression economics but operates under material constraint: the absence of tight peer comparables at identical stage and tier. Key uncertainties include the relevance of later-stage defense peers to pre-seed risk profile and the degree to which Galadyne's specific technology and market position warrant premium or discount to stage benchmarks. Further validation would require closer stage-matched comparable data.
